sections.title: CME & Continuing Education
sections.description: Stay current with continuing medical education resources, board preparation, and professional development opportunities for dermatologists.

items.name: AAD Continuing Medical Education
items.description: The American Academy of Dermatology offers CME courses, workshops, and self-assessment modules for board-certified dermatologists.

items.name: DermNet CME Resources
items.description: DermNet provides free educational content and clinical images useful for continuing education and teaching.

items.name: VisualDx
items.description: Clinical decision support with over 32,000 images spanning 1,300 diagnoses for diagnostic confidence.

items.name: Board Vitals Dermatology
items.description: Board review question banks for dermatology certification and recertification preparation.

items.name: Oakstone Dermatology CME
items.description: Dermatology CME courses and conferences featuring leading dermatology experts.

sections.title: Research & Journals
sections.description: Access leading dermatology journals, PubMed searches, and research databases for the latest clinical evidence.

items.name: Journal of the American Academy of Dermatology (JAAD)
items.description: The official journal of the AAD, publishing peer-reviewed original research, reviews, and clinical studies.

items.name: JAMA Dermatology
items.description: JAMA's dermatology specialty journal with high-impact research articles and clinical reviews.

items.name: British Journal of Dermatology
items.description: Leading international dermatology journal with global research perspectives.

items.name: PubMed Dermatology Search
items.description: Search the National Library of Medicine's database for dermatology research articles and clinical trials.

items.name: ClinicalTrials.gov - Dermatology
items.description: Find active and recruiting clinical trials in dermatology across the United States.

sections.title: Professional Organizations
sections.description: Connect with professional dermatology organizations for networking, advocacy, and career development.

items.name: American Academy of Dermatology (AAD)
items.description: The largest and most influential dermatology group in the United States with over 20,000 members.

items.name: Society for Investigative Dermatology (SID)
items.description: Promoting research in dermatology and cutaneous biology through scientific meetings and publications.

items.name: American Society for Dermatologic Surgery (ASDS)
items.description: The largest specialty organization for dermatologic surgery and cosmetic procedures.

items.name: Women's Dermatologic Society (WDS)
items.description: Supporting women dermatologists through mentorship, advocacy, and community service.

items.name: Skin of Color Society
items.description: Promoting awareness and excellence in dermatologic care for skin of color patients.

sections.title: Practice Management
sections.description: Resources for running an efficient dermatology practice, including EHR systems, billing, compliance, and marketing.

items.name: Dermatology EHR Solutions
items.description: Electronic health record systems designed specifically for dermatology workflows and documentation.

items.name: AAD Practice Management Center
items.description: Practice management resources from the AAD including coding, compliance, and regulatory guidance.

items.name: CMS Dermatology Billing Codes
items.description: Current CPT and ICD-10 coding resources for dermatology procedures and diagnoses.

items.name: OSHA Compliance for Medical Offices
items.description: Workplace safety and compliance guidelines for healthcare facilities and medical offices.

items.name: Healthcare Marketing Best Practices
items.description: Ethical marketing guidelines and digital presence strategies for medical practices.

sections.title: Events & Conferences
sections.description: Major dermatology conferences, annual meetings, and educational events happening this year and beyond.

items.name: AAD Annual Meeting 2026
items.description: The premier dermatology conference with thousands of attendees, featuring the latest research, workshops, and networking.

items.name: ASDS Annual Meeting
items.description: The ASDS Annual Meeting focuses on surgical and cosmetic dermatology innovations and techniques.

items.name: World Congress of Dermatology
items.description: The world's largest gathering of dermatologists, held every four years by the International League of Dermatological Societies.

items.name: Maui Derm
items.description: Focused dermatology conference covering clinical updates, new therapies, and practice innovations.

items.name: Orlando Dermatology Conference
items.description: Annual aesthetic and clinical dermatology conference with hands-on workshops and expert lectures.

sections.title: Clinical Guidelines
sections.description: Evidence-based clinical practice guidelines, treatment algorithms, and consensus statements from leading dermatology organizations.

items.name: AAD Clinical Guidelines
items.description: Official guidelines from the AAD covering acne, atopic dermatitis, psoriasis, skin cancer, and more.

items.name: NCCN Skin Cancer Guidelines
items.description: National Comprehensive Cancer Network guidelines for melanoma, basal cell, squamous cell, and Merkel cell carcinoma.

items.name: WHO Skin Disease Guidelines
items.description: World Health Organization guidelines on neglected tropical diseases and global dermatological conditions.

items.name: BAD Clinical Guidelines
items.description: British Association of Dermatologists evidence-based guidelines for common skin conditions.

items.name: European Dermatology Forum Guidelines
items.description: European consensus guidelines for diagnosis and treatment of dermatological diseases.
